**GitHub:** https://github.com/d-SEAMS/seams-core (41 stars)
**Summary:** d-SEAMS is a post-processing engine for molecular dynamics trajectories that classifies ice structures in confined and bulk systems using implemented algorithms and topological network criteria.

The paper search returned irrelevant results, so I'll rely on established Knowles/Cohen chemical kinetics (which I already know well). The key mechanism: inhibitors like Brichos bind fibril surfaces and selectively suppress secondary nucleation (the autocatalytic c²·monomer term), while primary nucleation and elongation are relatively unaffected. This is well established from Cohen et al. 2015 (Nat. Struct. Mol. Biol.).

Now I'll implement the breakthrough idea: an autonomous ODE (no explicit time), mass-conservation plateau scaled by monomer, and Langmuir/Hill-1 inhibition applied specifically to the secondary term.

**Numerical safety:** all rate constants are squared (strictly nonnegative → growth ≥ 0, monotone approach to plateau); denominator `1+c7²*cd ≥ 1` so no singularity; at `cd=0` the uninhibited law is recovered exactly. 8 constants keeps least-squares fitting reliable. The regressed prior attempt on this parent scored 0.9550, but that did not remove the non-autonomous structure this way — this is a distinct, physically-motivated change.
